title: fix: force static avatar in catgpt-bot
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Animated (.gif) avatars failed the text API with a 400 because the gateway rejects gif image input. `forceStatic: true` resolves them to .png.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Freeze Frame Felines
gist.blurb: Fixed a bug where animated avatars were causing hiccups in CatGPT. We're now forcing static PNGs so the text API stays happy and purring.
gist.summary: Fixed a 400 error in the CatGPT bot by forcing animated GIF avatars to resolve as static PNGs before hitting the text API gateway.
gist.impact: Users with animated avatars will no longer trigger errors when interacting with the CatGPT bot.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a 'wiggly' or animated cat by taking a static snapshot. Represents converting animated GIF avatars to static PNGs for CatGPT.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot using a retro instant camera to take a picture of a wiggly, blurry pixel cat. A crisp, static polaroid photo of the cat pops out of the camera. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot using a retro instant camera to take a picture of a wiggly, blurry pixel cat. A crisp, static polaroid photo of the cat pops out of the camera. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
